title: Marveen Autonomous Product Graph — Canonical Artifact Schema (draft)
description: AI-first, structured, schema-validated canonical objects for the Marveen Autonomous Product Graph. Long Markdown/HTML docs are ONLY projections generated from these objects and are NEVER authoritative. This schema is the shared foundation referenced by the lifecycle transition-table and the context-packet-contract authored by a peer agent; object-type names, edge `rel` names, and envelope field names are load-bearing and must stay stable across all three documents. Persisted in the SQLite sidecar store/product-graph.db, one row per object (envelope columns) + payload JSON blob; NEVER inside store/claudeclaw.db.
$comment: The root of a validated instance is exactly ONE canonical object. Validate every object against #/$defs/artifact then against the type-specific $def selected by `type` (see #/$defs/typeDispatch).

objectType.description: The 19 canonical object types. Every node in the Product Graph is exactly one of these.

lifecyclePhase.description: The Product LCM phase. Carried authoritatively by `product` and by `change` (the delta unit). NEVER derived from kanban status (principle 6).

edgeRel.description: Typed, directional edge relation (from THIS object -> the referenced object). The (rel, from-type, to_type) triples that are legal are enumerated in #/$defs/legalEdges and MUST be honored by writers; the peer agent's transition-table keys transitions off these same rel names.

to_id.description: id of the referenced canonical object. Must resolve to a live (non-invalidated unless rel=supersedes) object of `to_type`.

note.description: Optional human note; NOT authoritative, projection-only.

objectStatus.description: Object-scoped status. This is the artifact's OWN production/acceptance state and is DISTINCT from (a) kanban task-status, (b) lifecycle_phase, (c) gate_status, (d) decision_status (principle 6 separation).

gateStatus.description: Three-layer gate outcome for a gated object. Ordering is enforced by the Gate Engine: deterministic ALWAYS runs and must pass before the semantic (LLM verifier) runs (principle 1); the producing agent's execution_id may NEVER appear as the accepting actor (principle 2); human is only present for owner-decision phases (principle 9).

check_id.description: e.g. tsc_build, test_exit_code, route_reachable_200, fe_be_wired, artifact_hash_present, schema_valid

evidence_ref.description: id of a build/test/runtime_evidence object or a ledger event_id

verifier_agent.description: role of the LLM verifier; MUST differ from provenance.created_by (principle 2)

verified_hash.description: content_hash the verifier saw; if equal to current content_hash on a later pass, re-verification is skipped (principle 3)

rationale_ref.description: ledger event_id holding the verifier rationale (projection-only)

approval_id.description: id of the `approval` object carrying the owner decision

modelTier.description: Which model class produced/verified this artifact. Drives cost accounting and the autonomy of self-check. deepseek NEVER carries sensitive data-in-context (see architecture doc, model-tiering).

provenance.description: WHO/HOW this object was produced. The append-only ledger is the fuller trail; this is the inline, queryable summary. Memory/conversation-summary is NEVER cited here (principle 4).

created_by.description: Fleet agent role that produced the object.

execution_id.description: Idempotent execution ID (principle 7). Every side effect that produced this object ran under this ID; replaying the same execution_id is a no-op. Format: <phase>-<lineage_id>-<attempt>-<ulid>.

source_refs.description: ids of upstream canonical objects this artifact was derived from (redundant-but-queryable mirror of derives_from/traces_to/supported_by edges).

tokens.description: Per-object token accounting, fed by the Context Router / token-measurement methodology.

artifact.description: Common envelope shared by ALL 19 object types. Type-specific fields live under `payload` and are validated by the matching $def.

id.description: Globally unique object id (ULID). Immutable; a new version gets a NEW id and a supersedes edge to the old one.

lineage_id.description: Stable identity across versions/supersede. version=1 sets lineage_id=id by convention.

content_hash.description: sha256 over the canonical (sorted-key, edges included, provenance/gate excluded) serialization of `payload` + `edges`. The invalidation/re-verification engine keys off this: unchanged hash => no re-read/re-verify (principle 3).

lifecycle_phase.description: Authoritative ONLY on product and change; advisory/absent elsewhere.

supersedes.description: id of the object this one replaces (same lineage_id, version-1).

superseded_by.description: id of the object that replaced this one; set when this object is retired.

invalidated.description: Set true when an upstream source_ref hash changed (cascade invalidation). Invalidated objects are excluded from context packets and gates until re-derived.

typeDispatch.$comment: Applicators: pick the payload $def by `type`. Kept as documentation of the intended dispatch; a validator implementation applies the matching payload $def after #/$defs/artifact.

legalEdges.$comment: Reference table (not an enforced schema) of the legal (from_type, rel, to_type) triples. Writers and the Gate Engine enforce these; the peer transition-table and context-packet-contract MUST use identical rel names.

payload_product.description: Root product node (e.g. LumaSeat, MikroKonyv, QuickQuote, Zsibongo, DORA). One live per product lineage.

slug.description: stable key, matches kanban `project` and marveen-suite app dir, e.g. 'lumaseat'

deploy_target.description: pre-approved Render service id/name; deploys here are AUTOMATIC

kill_criteria_ref.description: id of the metric_definition whose breach proposes sunset

payload_opportunity.description: A scored market opportunity derived from market_facts. Maintained pipeline, revisited go/no-go (fixes AS-IS FM: June-only one-shot).

revisit_at.description: when the go/no-go should be re-evaluated

payload_market_fact.description: One atomic, sourced market/competitor claim. Delta-based (OpenSpec pattern): each fact is a discrete node so competitor knowledge accretes instead of full re-research. Links to the intel registry (idea_box/research deliverables/memories) via source_uri.

subject.description: competitor/segment/product slug the fact is about

stale_after.description: epoch after which the fact must be re-scouted

payload_hypothesis.description: A falsifiable belief about a product/opportunity, tested against runtime_evidence + metric_observations. Closes the AS-IS 'feedback loop runs on qualitative signals not measured ones' gap.

payload_business_definition.description: The single LIVE authoritative business brief per product (value-prop + unit-economics + GTM + success/kill criteria). Superseded, never edited-in-place; staleness is a first-class supersede, fixing AS-IS FM-22.

cogs_ref.description: CostOps per-product cost handle for margin awareness (fixes FM-24)

items.description: id of a metric_definition + breach threshold

accepted_intent_hash.description: content_hash of the owner-accepted intent; a material change to this requires an approval (autonomy matrix)

payload_change.description: The DELTA unit (OpenSpec pattern): a single coherent product change that flows through the lifecycle. Carries its own lifecycle_phase (envelope). Kanban epic mirrors this 1:1 but is NOT authoritative (principle 6).

intent.description: what user/business outcome this change delivers

gate_profile.description: named profile selecting which deterministic+semantic checks apply, e.g. 'ui_backend_integration_full'

kanban_epic_id.description: mirror kanban_cards.id (external); provenance link only, not source of truth

blocked_on_approval.description: id of a pending approval when the phase is a human-gated one

rollback_ref.description: id of the deployment to roll back to; required before deploy phase

payload_requirement.description: One testable requirement of a change. traces_to a business_definition; verified by tests + runtime_evidence. The 'AC field' the AS-IS audit found missing.

surface.description: which surfaces the requirement touches; drives the fe_be_wired deterministic check (fixes FM-06 implemented-not-integrated)

payload_scenario.description: Concrete given/when/then scenario specifying a requirement (Spec Kit progressive disclosure: scenarios expand requirements only when needed).

payload_decision.description: A recorded decision (design choice, phase step-back, go/no-go). decision_status is a SEPARATE axis from object status (principle 6). Fixes AS-IS FM-01 (agreed-not-recorded, e.g. nav sidebar->top-tabs).

requires_owner.description: true when it falls in the human-approval autonomy bucket

payload_design_node.description: A UX/UI design artifact (screen/flow/component/graphics-parity note) designing a requirement. Graphics-parity is measured against the thick proto (the product-truth), not a landing.

proto_ref.description: path/handle to the thick proto this parity-checks against

payload_task.description: An implementation unit implementing a requirement. Mirrors a kanban_cards row for dispatch/visualization; the kanban STATUS is work-tracking, NEVER lifecycle (principle 6). Carries the task lease + idempotency for the duplication-guard.

kanban_card_id.description: mirror kanban_cards.id via POST /api/kanban; provenance link only

lease.description: Task lease + duplication-guard (LangGraph idempotency): only the holder of a live lease may transition the task; expiry allows safe re-dispatch without double-work.

dedup_key.description: stable key = <change_lineage>:<requirement_lineage>:<task_kind>; a second create with the same dedup_key is rejected

payload_test.description: A test verifying a requirement / covering a scenario. Its RESULT is a deterministic gate input (principle 1); a test authored by the implementing agent may not by itself accept the requirement (principle 2).

authored_by_role.description: must differ from the accepting verifier (principle 2)

payload_build.description: A build of a change. Source->build provenance: commit sha + build command + artifact digest, so 'the running bundle contains the merged change' is checkable (fixes AS-IS FM-07/08 gaps).

artifact_digest.description: hash of the produced bundle/dist for source-vs-build verify

payload_deployment.description: A deployment of a build to an environment. Deploy to a PRE-APPROVED env is AUTOMATIC; any other target needs an approval. Provenance ties env+service+commit for reachability checks.

build_ref.description: id of the build object deployed

payload_runtime_evidence.description: Observed runtime fact about a deployment: feature reachability, smoke result, health. The keystone the AS-IS chain was missing (FM-09 deployed-not-reachable). evidences a requirement => flips it to 'reachable'.

evidence_uri.description: screenshot path / response capture / ledger event_id

payload_metric_definition.description: Defines a business KPI (signups/activation/conversion/retention/revenue/margin) with baseline+target+threshold. The measurement layer the AS-IS audit calls the keystone gap (section 14).

threshold.description: gap-analysis alarm level; breach can auto-propose optimization/repricing/sunset changes

payload_metric_observation.description: One measured value for a metric_definition at a time, on a deployment. instance_of a metric_definition. Feeds KPI-gap analysis + hypothesis evaluation.

adapter_run_id.description: idempotent adapter execution id; dedup by (metric_definition, window_start, window_end)

payload_product_review.description: Periodic product health review aggregating metric_observations into a verdict + proposed next-lifecycle action (repricing|scaling|pivot|sunset). The PROPOSAL is automatic; enacting a pivot/sunset/repricing-go-live needs an approval.

proposal_change_ref.description: id of the auto-created change carrying the proposal

payload_approval.description: The ONLY human interrupt point (principle 9). Records an owner decision for an autonomy-matrix gated action. Created by the Approval Broker; resolved by Istvan via Telegram/dashboard. No side effect in the gated bucket proceeds without a resolved approval.

resolved_by.description: only the owner may resolve; bus 'from' is not trusted (AS-IS: no sender-auth)

escalation_deadline.description: deterministic escalation-clock; on expiry the gated action stays blocked and re-pings (fixes AS-IS FM-15 no escalation-clock)
